items.headline: Council reviews $2026-27 capital spending plans
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held a study session on April 14 to examine the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines major infrastructure and facility projects planned for the city. This is worth tracking if you care about what physical improvements or construction projects are coming to Mountain View.

items.headline: New tenant protections ordinance introduced for Sunnyvale
items.summary: The City Council introduced changes to Chapter 19.71 of the municipal code affecting the Residential Tenant Protections Program. This ordinance could impact housing rights and landlord-tenant rules for Sunnyvale residents.

items.headline: City releases Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan
items.summary: San José presented a status report on its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, which outlines improvements to library services and physical spaces. This plan could affect access to library services and facilities across the city.

items.headline: Downtown building seeks historic status upgrade and floor area bonus
items.summary: The building at 405 Kipling Street is requesting elevation from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status, which would unlock 5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for renovation and seismic improvements. This decision could enable substantial rehabilitation of a downtown building and expand its capacity.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop property at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road from commercial use into an eight-story mixed-use building with 167 residential units (including 28 below-market-rate units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents a significant change to that area of the city and will require City Council approval.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8, 2026, with no new hearing date yet scheduled. Residents affected by or interested in this development should watch for a new notice when the hearing is rescheduled.

items.headline: City Council authorizes construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully visible in the agenda. This represents a committed capital project moving forward for Los Gatos.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with consultant agreement
items.summary: City Council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project. This safety project aims to improve traffic flow and coordination between vehicle and rail traffic on Bascom Avenue.
